frozen_components: digit classifier (during Symbol Grounding Loss training)
architecture.summary: The system combines unsupervised clustering (InfoGAN), knowledge distillation into a digit classifier (LeNet), a differentiable MAXSAT solver (SATNet), and a learned permutation-alignment mechanism. Information flows from raw images → clustering → distilled classifier → SATNet constraint layer with a novel Symbol Grounding Loss that learns the permutation between pre-trained and label encodings without access to input labels. An optional proofreader layer (linear transformation) is applied post-training. The architecture solves ungrounded Visual MAXSAT problems where only output variable labels are available.

biblio.title: Techniques for Symbol Grounding with SATNet

claims.statement: Our method achieves full accuracy even with a harder problem setup that prevents any label leakage, improving the state of the art on Ungrounded Visual Sudoku from 0% to 64.8% total board accuracy
Our method achieves 98.4 ± 0.2% per-cell accuracy on Ungrounded Visual Sudoku
Our method achieves 98.9 ± 0.1% visual accuracy (input cell classification) on Ungrounded Visual Sudoku
With proofreading, our method achieves 64.8 ± 3.0% total board accuracy on Ungrounded Visual Sudoku, compared to 62.8 ± 3.2% without proofreading
InfoGAN clustering achieves 89.6 ± 7.7% clustering accuracy on the digit clustering task
Distilled LeNet achieves 86.2 ± 13.5% digit clustering accuracy, distilled from InfoGAN (89.6 ± 7.7%), with 1,049,080 parameters vs InfoGAN's 7,307,997 parameters
Our system requires roughly at least 88% clustering accuracy in order for the rest of the pipeline to progress; performance shows a sharp drop below this threshold
4 out of 10 runs of the pre-training pipeline converge to a clustering below the 88% threshold necessary to ground symbols, indicating sensitivity to random seed initialization
